mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and residential housing starts indicates a broad-based demand shock that reduces corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.0 percent, rising real activity measured by GDP growth forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL implies annual inflation above 3.0 percent
failure_condition: The mechanism reverses direction when inflation exceeds 3.0 percent, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between high-inflation and low-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth periods consistently yield negative or flat forward returns, or the rolling correlation between monthly payroll changes and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve credit to depository institutions falls below 450 billion dollars, it signals a withdrawal of bank liquidity that tightens financial conditions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when RSAFS breaches the 450 billion dollar threshold, or the signal predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while the federal funds rate simultaneously increases indicates supply-driven growth outpacing monetary tightening, which historically supports risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term becomes statistically insignificant, or the signal predicts negative returns in more than 55% of test windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated unemployment above 4.5 percent, any acceleration in GDP growth acts as a powerful mean-reversion catalyst that boosts equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The positive return relationship disappears when unemployment exceeds 5.0 percent, or forward returns are indistinguishable from baseline during high-unemployment GDP acceleration episodes.
